title: tecticornia arborea
intro: Tecticornia arborea, also known as coastal glasswort, is a type of succulent plant native to coastal areas in Australia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Tecticornia arborea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its ability to ease respiratory congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
